design_philosophy.core_loop: Shoot enemies and obstacles with light gun peripheral while managing power gauge depletion; navigate branching level paths to reach boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Power gauge management—firing rapidly depletes the gauge, causing long pauses that increase vulnerability to enemy attacks
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: First-person shooting adventure as Mario riding Yoshi, using the Super Scope peripheral to save Jewelry Land from Bowser's invasion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unique light gun shooter in the Mario franchise with peripheral-dependent gameplay; deliberately designed to showcase SNES Mode 7 3D effects and rehabilitate the Super Scope's commercial viability

difficulty.lives_system: Extra life earned by collecting 60 coins; lose lives when enemies deplete health bar, falling into pits with fewer than three coins, or failing to complete level within time limit; losing all lives results in game over forcing restart
difficulty.checkpointing: Levels must be completed within a time limit; failure to do so results in life loss. Branching paths within levels offer optional routes but converge at boss encounter
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Cheat code unlocked upon completion increases difficulty level for subsequent playthroughs, enabling replay with enhanced challenge

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups (mushrooms, flowers, stars) provide extra health, firepower, and invincibility; some power-ups exclusive to boss battles
progression.unlock_mechanism: 12 levels selected from overworld map; progression locked behind completion of each level's boss encounter. Difficulty modifier unlocked via cheat code after initial completion
replayability_hooks: Leaderboard system encourages score tracking and multiple playthroughs
Branching level paths offer different enemies and prizes on alternate routes
Unlockable difficulty enhancement via cheat code extends challenge replay value

world_building_techniques: Jewelry Land setting mirrors Mushroom Kingdom with distinct aesthetic
Established villain Bowser and Koopalings as antagonists using mecha piloted by Iggy
Princess Peach quest framing connecting to broader Mario lore

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating mecha-based encounters featuring Koopalings and Bowser; larger scaled versions of normal enemies that require sustained firepower and pattern recognition

faction_indicators: Bowser and Koopalings as unified invasion force
Kingdom of Jewelry Land and its captured rulers as oppressed faction
Mario and Yoshi as heroes of resistance
danger_signals: Earthquake splitting Jewelry Land in two signals kingdom-wide catastrophe
Theft of 12 magic gems removes kingdom's protective enchantments
Koopaling mecha weapons signal technological military threat
environmental_storytelling: Jewelry Land's split geography reflects earthquake devastation
Enemy placement and density indicate Bowser's occupation and control
Branching level paths suggest exploration of invaded territory with pockets of resistance
